'''Varada Five''' was a terrestrial astronomical object located in the [[Varada system/Legends|Varada system]],<ref name="Polyhedron" /> a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Varada sector/Legends|Varada sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Varada Five was home to the headquarters of the Varada [[Sector/Legends|sector]] branch of the [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]],<ref name="Polyhedron" /> a [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]]-run conglomerate based on the<ref name="GATORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> [[Brema sector/Legends|Brema sector]]<ref name="EAOC Sullust">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Sullust system/Legends|Sullust system]]</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]].<ref name="GATORW" />

During that [[Time/Legends|time]], the Sullustan [[Tyanna Tymb]] was appointed to the position of the top operating executive, or [[Top OpEx]], of SoroSuub Corporation's Varada sector holdings. As such, Tymb was charged with controlling the corporation's headquarters on Varada Five as well as coordinating the expansion of her employer's interests in the Varada [[Star system/Legends|system]] and the rest of the sector.<ref name="Polyhedron" />

==Behind the scenes==
Varada Five was introduced in "[[Adversaries: Characters for the Star Wars: New Republic Campaign]]," a [[roleplaying game]] source article authored by [[Bill Slavicsek]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in the [[Polyhedron 97|ninety-seventh issue]] of the ''[[Polyhedron]]'' magazine in [[July]] [[1994]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Polyhedron" /> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Varada system, and therefore Varada Five, in grid square I-19.<ref name="TEA Varada" />
